pi_items.source: Verified across setup_task.sh (line 14), export_result.sh (line 5), and task.json description

Standard 24-tooth involute spur gear specification confirmed in task description and verifier.py. 24 teeth is within standard gear design range (minimum 17 teeth for 20° pressure angle to avoid undercutting).
Module 2.0 mm is a standard metric module per ISO and ANSI standards. Confirmed in all scripts and verifier calculations. Used to compute pitch diameter (48 mm) and tip diameter (52 mm).

20° is the standard and most common pressure angle for modern involute gears per KHK Gears, SDPSI, and gear design standards. Web search confirms 20° is preferred over older 14.5° due to better tooth strength and less undercutting.
Extrusion height/thickness of 10 mm confirmed in task description, verifier.py (line 41, EXPECTED_THICKNESS = 10.0), export_result.sh, and setup_task.sh. Used to calculate bounding box Z dimension.
Central bore radius of 6 mm (equals 12 mm diameter) confirmed in task description and all scripts. Hole volume = π × 6² × 10 ≈ 1131 mm³, matching verifier comment on line 47.
Calculated using standard involute gear formula: tip_diameter = m(z + 2) = 2.0 × (24 + 2) = 52.0 mm. Independent Python calculation confirms this value.
Calculated using standard formula: pitch_diameter = m × z = 2.0 × 24 = 48.0 mm. Confirmed by independent calculation.
Calculated using standard formula: root_diameter = m(z - 2.5) = 2.0 × 21.5 = 43.0 mm. Confirmed by independent calculation.

privileged_info_summary: This task requires creating a standard involute spur gear with verified specifications: 24 teeth, Module 2.0 mm, 20° pressure angle (standard for modern gears), 10 mm extrusion height, and 6 mm bore radius. All metadata values are mathematically consistent and comply with ISO/ANSI gear design standards. The expected bounding box is 52×52×10 mm (calculated as tip diameter × tip diameter × height). The gear is computationally synthetic, generated using FreeCAD's involute gear tool with no external data source.
